steps.title: Backsolving by testing the candidate
steps.content:
Backsolving by testing the candidate (C(d)=56d-28):
For $d=1$, expected cost is 56 dollars.
$$C(1)=56\cdot1-28=28$$
Since $28\neq56$, the remaining alternative fails.

steps.title: Backsolving by testing the candidate
steps.content:
Backsolving by testing the candidate (C(d)=28d+28):
For $d=1$, expected cost is 56 dollars.
$$C(1)=28\cdot1+28=56$$
For $d=2$, expected cost is 56 dollars plus 28 dollars, or 84 dollars.
$$C(2)=28\cdot2+28=84$$
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
